text: Right now the cleanest reusable-skill comparison is anthropics/skills versus shanraisshan/claude-code-best-practice. One gives the official baseline, the other gives sharper practice around memory, hooks, and verification.
phuryn/pm-skills matters beyond product management. It is a strong example of turning repeated work into a transferable skill pack that can move between projects.
If you need a discipline baseline for coding agents, read official repos like anthropics/skills together with practice rules from shanraisshan/claude-code-best-practice, not in isolation.
Spec-driven workflow, context engineering, and MCP retrieval keep converging on the same lesson: do not keep everything in one giant AGENTS.md file, split knowledge into layers.
